items.title: CPT2 inhibition enhances selective autophagy and proliferation in colorectal cancer via GPAT4-dependent glycerophospholipid biosynthesis
items.author: Juan Li
items.content: CPT2 downregulation promotes colorectal cancer proliferation by enhancing GPAT4-mediated glycerophospholipid biosynthesis and activating selective autophagy.

items.title: Midgut microbiota diversity and interactions shift when field Aedes aegypti mosquitoes carry the wMel strain of Wolbachia pipientis
items.author: Márcio G. Pavan
items.content: The analysis of field-caught Aedes aegypti from Brazil through 16S rRNA amplicon-sequencing (V3-V4 region) evidenced that Wolbachia wMel strain reduces the bacterial diversity of mosquito’s midgut and reshapes microbial interactions.

items.title: Decoding adult murine pancreatic islet cell diversity through cell type-resolved proteomics and phosphoproteomics
items.author: Raphaël Scharfmann
items.content: Here, the authors employ cell sorting and sensitive mass spectrometry to generate a comprehensive proteomic and phosphoproteomic resource, revealing inflammation responses and cell-specific signaling, advancing our understanding of islet biology in health and disease.

items.title: Cryo-EM structure of the human Derlin-1/p97 complex reveals a hexameric channel in ERAD
items.author: Yu Cao
items.content: Structural insights into the Derlin-1/p97 complex reveal a dynamic 6:6 architecture that expands the ERAD retrotranslocation pathway, highlighting ATPase-powered plasticity in processing bulky substrates.

items.title: Dual roles of salivary proteins in feeding and silk fiber coating in the spider mite Tetranychus urticae
items.author: Takeshi Suzuki
items.content: Spider mites produce adhesive silk fibers for web spinning. This study reveals that proteins previously thought to be silk components are salivary proteins with dual roles in feeding and silk fiber coating.

items.title: Diffusion trajectory of atypical morphological development in autism spectrum disorder
items.author: Xujun Duan
items.content: Using Kullback–Leibler divergence to analyze distribution deviations in brain morphology between ASDs and TDCs, this study reveals a shift from gray matter overgrowth to delayed maturation, with atypically developing regions constrained by intrinsic functional networks.

items.title: Mapping the temporal landscape of breast cancer using epigenetic entropy
items.author: Marc D. Ryser
items.content: The mitotic ages of breast tumors can be inferred from fluctuating CpG DNA methylation, with higher epigenetic entropy in older cancers.

items.title: OPTN protects retinal ganglion cells and ameliorates neuroinflammation in optic neuropathies
items.author: Feng Tian
items.content: OPTN dysfunction induces short-term astrogliosis and long-term microglial activation, driving IOP-independent RGC loss that contributes to normal tension and hypertensive glaucoma; OPTN overexpression alleviates neuroinflammation and neurodegeneration.
